title: SystemVerilog - ports without type declaration not equivalent to logic
body:
The following program fails to compile:
```systemverilog
module test(output a, input b);
    always_comb a = b;
endmodule
```
I get the following error message:
```
test.sv:2: error: a is not a valid l-value in test.
test.sv:1:      : a is declared here as wire.
Elaboration failed
```
If I add `logic` to port declaration, everything works fine:
```systemverilog
module test(output logic a, input b);
    always_comb a = b;
endmodule
```
This is incorrect - in SystemVerilog there should be no difference between not declaring a type and declaring it as `logic`.
code_blocks:
test.sv:2: error: a is not a valid l-value in test.
test.sv:1:      : a is declared here as wire.
Elaboration failed
